title: Scope host-granted permissions to each tool's real need
description: Grant each MCP tool only the filesystem, network, and command rights its declared function requires, gated by host consent, not a blanket allow.
body: Use when a host decides what an MCP tool may touch. Do not grant a read-only lookup tool write or shell access, and avoid auto-approving a tool that requests broad filesystem or network scope. Actions: read the tool's declared schema and side effects, map them to the minimum rights, present the specific scope to the host/user for consent, and deny anything beyond it; record the granted scope per tool. Validation signals include a consent log per tool invocation and a test that an out-of-scope operation is rejected. Risk boundary: a tool requesting unrestricted shell or filesystem access is a privilege-escalation risk; deny by default and escalate to a human for explicit approval rather than widening scope automatically.
